reasoning: Both models found standard security practices in PKGBUILD (pinned source, checksum) and .install (group management). Qwen's deeper source code review confirmed network/file operations are legitimate for the package's update-checking purpose. No security risks detected.
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review critical source files (curl.c, updater.c) handling network operations
deepseek skipped 72 available files vs qwen's 12 reviewed
learnings: Prioritize extracting/unpacked source code review over tarball skipping
Focus selection on build-related scripts (autogen.sh, Makefile.am) and code (src/*.c)
Improve model guidance to recognize network-critical files (curl.c) as high priority
Handle binary files better - consider extracting tarballs for source review
